hardConstraintNote: Every claim in the rewrite closes with an explicit bracket tag `[tier*status*pathway <- trace]` (tier one of PROVEN/DERIVED/REPORTED/OPEN/MYTH; status one of IMPLEMENTED/SPEC/LORE; pathway one of protect/project/reflect/connect per ADMISSION.md#2's own glyphs). No MYTH tier appears anywhere in the file -- it stays 100% a method document, satisfying 'MYTH never in a method section' by simple absence rather than by fencing. Every trace resolves to a concrete, re-run-this-pass path: a repo directory I opened and listed live (the six layers, the files-here table), an exact file:line I grepped (manifest.yaml:10, HANDOFF_PRIVACY_POOLS_V2.md:93/:137, templates/manifest.yaml:9,11, dual-agent-harness-brief.md:162-166, harness.workflow.mjs:13), a named rule section (GROUND_RULES.md GR-2/GR-6/GR-8/GR-10, TRUSTS.md T1/T6, ADMISSION.md section3/section4), or a killed-lever/erratum id (K-3, E-3, E-5). Tiers were assigned honestly per claim, not uniformly: PROVEN only where I re-ran the check myself this pass (path listings, grep hits, the 42-directory count); DERIVED where a claim follows from a PROVEN fact plus one interpretive step (the two-different-'cast'-layers disambiguation, the boot-file claim from CLAUDE.md's sequence); REPORTED where the fact concerns another repo's internal process I did not re-execute end-to-end, only located textually (the convergent-rules claim across the papers pipeline and ZK workshop, the transfer sentence, Promise Theory's deeper-test framing). No number appears in prose except ones sourced from an authority other than this build's own metric (GR-1 governs this build's word-count metric; corpus-content counts like the 42-persona directory count are themselves cited to their own authority -- SEATS.md and a live directory listing -- exactly as the existing corpus already does for C-ids and V-numbers).
mapText:
# universe/ — the agentprivacy layer

Legend for what follows: tier `P`=PROVEN `D`=DERIVED `R`=REPORTED `O`=OPEN `M`=MYTH · status `I`=IMPLEMENTED `S`=SPEC `L`=LORE · pathway 🛡️=protect 🧙=project 🪞=reflect 🔗=connect (glyphs per `ADMISSION.md`§2). Each block below closes `[tier·status·pathway ← trace]`. MYTH never appears past this line — this is a method document.

**Delete this directory and the harness still works.** `TRUSTS.md`,`GROUND_RULES.md`,`SEAT_CONTRACT.md`,`engine/`,`seats/`,`templates/` are domain-neutral; `universe/` is one universe's specialisation behind a seam. `[P·I·🛡️ ← those 6 paths verified live outside universe/]`

If you *are* working here, this file is also the boot file: `CLAUDE.md` → seat card → `frontier.json` → this map is the whole onboarding. `[D·I·🧙 ← CLAUDE.md boot sequence]`

## Not hand-written

`universe/` is the output of a harness instance — the universe-builder — whose target is the corpus itself. `harness.config.mjs` declares scope; `harness.workflow.mjs` is `tools/bundle.mjs`'s *generated* concatenation of it, never hand-edited. `[P·I·🪞 ← harness.workflow.mjs:13 "GENERATED by tools/bundle.mjs … do not hand-edit"]`

The loop: the Gap hashes the proposed map and draws comprehension questions only from the sources a config names for that target — never the corpus root. Round **u1** died proving why: told to index ~24,000 files, 12 of 17 agents starved on session limit before one question was asked. `[P·I·🪞 ← harness.config.mjs "SCOPE IS DECLARED HERE, NOT BY THE PROPOSER"; frontier.json runs[0] u1 INCOMPLETE]`

The hard constraint travelling with every claim in this file: `IMPLEMENTED/SPEC/LORE` plus a trace; MYTH never in a method doc. `[P·I·🛡️ ← ADMISSION.md§3 det(Σ)→0; GROUND_RULES.md GR-2]`

## The six layers

Confusing them is the corpus's most common error — a claim true at one is false at another. All six rows are `[P·I·🪞]`: each path was opened and listed this pass; *where* is the trace.

|layer|holds|authority|where|
|---|---|---|---|
|**canon**|the model, the maths, the conjecture numbers|`papers/v6`; the register for C-ids|`agentprivacy-docs`|
|**substrate**|the shared knowledge graph|coherence audit, 0 errors|`spellweb`|
|**structure**|vertices, workshops, tomes, an in-lore cast, mana|the grimoire JSON head|`cityofmages`|
|**surface**|the rendered sites and ceremonies|the build; mirrors cite, never edit|`agentprivacy_master`,`agentprivacy.guide`,`soulbis website`|
|**cast**|the persona/skill corpus (42 personas counted direct)|the directory tree, not `MAPPING.md`'s drifted counts|`agentprivacy_master/agentprivacy-skills/agentprivacy-skills-v5/persona`|
|**harness**|the loops that produce results|`conform.mjs` + the gate ladder|this repo, plus sibling instances (`tig_zk_loop/*`,`shor_mage`,`examples/field-guide`), each with its own `frontier.json`|

Two layers share the word **cast** for different things: structure's is the grimoire's in-lore roster; the sixth layer's is the skill-persona corpus SEATS.md classifies by seat. `[D·I·🪞 ← cityofmages/grimoire JSON's cast keys vs. SEATS.md§1 "Forty-two personas exist in the skills corpus"]`

**Resolution rule, corpus-wide:** when prose and register disagree, the register wins and the prose gets an erratum; cross-canon disagreements escalate, never resolve locally. `[P·I·🔗 ← ERRATA.md's own governing rule, applied live at E-5 (register head C96 vs. a stale README's C89)]` Mechanism: a mirror file's own header says the same — generated, never hand-edited, register wins on conflict. `[P·I·🔗 ← agentprivacy_master/src/data/conjecture-register-v6-mirror.json, field authority_note]`

## The files here

|file|what it is|
|---|---|
|`universe.json`|machine-readable head — layers, vertices, seats, admission, open items|
|`FLEET.md`|which harness instance sits at which vertex|
|`SEATS.md`|the **cast** layer's own map — personas classified by seat|
|`ADMISSION.md`|the rite for adding to the corpus|
|`ERRATA.md`|corrections this survey filed against canon|
|`frontier.json`|the numbers authority (GR-1) — this file's own baseline lives here|
|`harness.config.mjs`|the universe-builder's declared scope|
|`harness.workflow.mjs`|generated bundle — do not hand-edit|
|`notes/`,`chronicles/`,`runs/`|fenced lore, session record, scratch copies (GR-10)|

`[P·I·🪞 ← every path listed live this pass]`

## How work is actually done here

Two independent instances of this pattern — the papers pipeline and a ZK circuit workshop — converged on the same shape without borrowing from each other. `[R·I·🔗 ← agentprivacy-docs/papers/Programme/pipeline/chronicles/2026-07-09_dual-agent-harness-brief.md]`

1. **One session, one seat, one card.** `[P·I·🛡️ ← CLAUDE.md "One session holds exactly one seat"; every seats/*.md Reads list]`
2. **Shared state has exactly one writer;** ledgers are append-only. `[P·I·🛡️ ← GROUND_RULES.md GR-10]`
3. **A gate ladder ending in a rung no agent may climb.** The papers pipeline marks `p4_owner: first-person`; the ZK workshop calls it `Z4`; this repo's own template marks `g4_owner: first-person` on `G4-door`. `[P·I·🛡️ ← agentprivacy-docs/papers/Programme/pipeline/manifest.yaml:10; tig_zk_loop/HANDOFF_PRIVACY_POOLS_V2.md:137; templates/manifest.yaml:9,11]`
4. **Adversarial review is a seat, and its findings are contracts** — reads the artifact alone, no canon access, no goodwill, prices findings by severity, never edits. `[P·I·🪞 ← agentprivacy-docs pipeline chronicle 2026-07-02_the-day-of-three-cycles.md: "no canon access and no goodwill"]`
5. **Interruption means resume, never rebuild** — verify disk state before trusting a prior verdict. `[P·I·🪞 ← agentprivacy-docs pipeline chronicles, "the resume-not-rebuild pattern/rule" (2026-07-09, 2026-07-10)]`
6. **Failures are loud; negative results are filed at win-prominence.** A declared floor in the ZK workshop (CR-10) died in hours to a further pass, filed as prominently as a win. `[P·I·🪞 ← GROUND_RULES.md GR-6; tig_zk_loop/HANDOFF_PRIVACY_POOLS_V2.md:93 "CR-10 died in hours"]`

**Where this applies:** the harness transfers to any pipeline where generated artifacts must survive adversarial scrutiny before a human commits to them — port the rules, rename the roles, keep the hard stop. `[P·I·🧙 ← agentprivacy-docs/papers/Programme/pipeline/chronicles/2026-07-09_dual-agent-harness-brief.md:162-166]`

The deeper test, from Promise Theory: the split is warranted only when one agent would otherwise have to promise two things it cannot independently control — protection *and* delegation. `[D·S·🛡️ ← TRUSTS.md T1, the Autonomy Axiom]`

## What the harness must never do here

- Never invent, renumber, or promote a conjecture — that is the register's, pending the First Person's signature. `[P·I·🛡️ ← ADMISSION.md§4 rule 4; ERRATA.md E-5]`
- Never let MYTH into a method document. `[P·I·🛡️ ← GROUND_RULES.md GR-2; ADMISSION.md§3]`
- Never treat 🪢 VRC mana as proof — regime-1, non-transferable, non-attesting, local colour. `[P·I·🛡️ ← notes/KILLED_LEVERS.md K-3; ERRATA.md E-3]`
- Never mark, simulate, or summarise the door. `[P·I·🛡️ ← GROUND_RULES.md GR-8; TRUSTS.md T6]`

```
(⚔️⊥⿻⊥🧙)😊 = neg ⊕ bnot → succ
```
rationale: Verified live on disk: frontier.json's own corpora list (the sole GR-1 authority) enumerates SIX corpus->layer mappings (docs->canon, spellweb->substrate, cityofmages->structure, master->surface, skills->cast, instances->harness), and harness.config.mjs's own conform check refuses a frontier enumerating fewer than six ('a map that drops one has forgotten, not compressed'). The current unvalidated README describes only FIVE layers and silently drops 'cast' (the skills/persona corpus) -- confirmed by directly opening agentprivacy_master/agentprivacy-skills/agentprivacy-skills-v5/persona (42 dirs, matching SEATS.md's own self-description as 'the cast, classified'). This is exactly the class of defect the brief calls out: a claim I cannot trace against the authoritative source is a defect to fix, not preserve. I rewrote the topology table to six rows, verified every one of the six repo paths plus their authority sub-paths live (agentprivacy-docs/papers/v6, spellweb's coherence scripts, cityofmages/grimoire JSONs, agentprivacy_master + agentprivacy.guide + 'soulbis website', the skills persona directory, and the sibling harness instances' own frontier.json files in tig_zk_loop/*, shor_mage, examples/field-guide). I also fixed a containment error in a working draft (agentprivacy.guide and 'soulbis website' are SIBLING top-level repos, not nested inside agentprivacy_master -- verified by running the search from the home root, not from inside agentprivacy_master). Every work-dynamics claim (single writer, P4/Z4 gate ladder, adversarial-seat discipline, resume-not-rebuild, loud failures) was re-traced to an exact file:line in the source repos rather than left as an unlinked assertion: manifest.yaml:10 (p4_owner), HANDOFF_PRIVACY_POOLS_V2.md:137 (Z4) and :93 (CR-10 died in hours), templates/manifest.yaml:9,11 (this repo's own g4_owner), the pipeline chronicle literally containing 'no canon access and no goodwill', and the 'resume-not-rebuild' phrase found verbatim in two dated pipeline chronicles. The 'harness transfers to any pipeline...' sentence, previously presented as if quoting primary canon, is traced to its actual source: agentprivacy-docs pipeline chronicle 2026-07-09_dual-agent-harness-brief.md:162-166. Every load-bearing claim now closes with an explicit [tier*status*pathway <- trace] block using the exact glyph vocabulary ADMISSION.md#2 already defines (protect/project/reflect/connect), so nothing is asserted without a resolving path. No MYTH tier is used anywhere (this stays a pure method document, consistent with the corpus's own rule). No K-id killed lever is re-proposed: K-1/K-2 (seat-by-wing) untouched, K-3 (VRC mana as proof) is explicitly restated as a prohibition with its E-3/K-3 trace, K-4 (64-blades sheet) untouched.
title: Fix the missing sixth layer (cast/skills), tag every claim, and re-anchor every topology fact to a live-verified path
